ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station above sea level */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed on the station */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the station (e.g., active, inactive, under maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_measurements" (
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather measurement */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station where the data was collected */,
  "date_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the measurement */,
  "max_temperature_f" REAL /* Maximum temperature in Fahrenheit */,
  "mean_temperature_f" REAL /* Mean temperature in Fahrenheit */,
  "min_temperature_f" REAL /* Minimum temperature in Fahrenheit */,
  "max_humidity" INTEGER /* Maximum humidity as a percentage */,
  "mean_humidity" INTEGER /* Mean humidity as a percentage */,
  "min_humidity" INTEGER /* Minimum humidity as a percentage */,
  "mean_sea_level_pressure_in" REAL /* Mean sea level pressure in inches of mercury */,
  "mean_wind_speed_mph" REAL /* Mean wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "cloud_cover" INTEGER /* Cloud cover as a percentage */,
  "events" TEXT /* Weather events (e.g., Rain, Snow) */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Method or device used to collect the data */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "timestamp_utc" TEXT /* UTC timestamp to standardize time zones */,
  "location_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the location data (e.g., GPS accuracy in meters) */,
  "sensor_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for the sensor used to collect the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("measurement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_measurements_events FOREIGN KEY ("events") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_measurements_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., weather analyst, data scientist, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User account status (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "measurement_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather measurement being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the measurement */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the measurement was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client used for access */,
  "session_id" TEXT /* Unique session identifier for the user */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access event in seconds */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location from which the access was made (e.g., city, country) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_measurement_id FOREIGN KEY ("measurement_id") REFERENCES weather_measurements ("measurement_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather event */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the weather event */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the weather event */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the weather event */,
  "estimated_impact" TEXT /* Estimated impact of the weather event */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Alert level for the weather event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)
